rulings.Case Title: BETTY JANISKEWSKI VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 22, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: SHANE BRANDON et al VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 22, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant U.S. Borax, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs¿ claims against it are barred by the statute of limitations. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs were not genuinely ignorant of defendant¿s true name or the facts rendering defendant liable any earlier than when they amended their complaint in March 2011 to substitute defendant in place of a Doe. This is not an impermissible motion for reconsideration. (See Community Memorial Hospital v. County of Venture (1996) 50 Cal.App.4th 199, 204-205.) Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
